ably: adverb. In an able manner; with great ability.
abo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Abon.
aby: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aneme Wake.
alb: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Albanian.
alp: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Alune.
aryl: noun. Any univalent organic radical derived from an aromatic hydrocarbon by removing a hydrogen atom.
baby: noun. A very young human, particularly from birth to a couple of years old or until walking is fully mastered.
bal: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Baluchi.
bap: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bantawa.
bar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bavarian.
barb: noun. The point that stands backward in an arrow, fishhook, etc., to prevent it from being easily extracted.
blob: noun. A shapeless or amorphous mass; a vague shape or amount, especially of a liquid or semisolid substance; a clump, group or collection
boa: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bora.
boar: noun. A wild boar (Sus scrofa), the wild ancestor of the domesticated pig.
bob: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aweer.
bola: noun. A throwing weapon made of weights on the ends of a cord.
bolar: adjective. Of, relating to, or similar to, bole or clay; clayey.
bop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bonkiman.
bora: noun. An initiation ceremony for males among the Aboriginal people of New South Wales.
boral: noun. aluminium borotartrate, an astringent and antiseptic.
boy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bodo (Central Africa).
boyar: noun. A member of a rank of aristocracy (second only to princes) in Russia, Ukraine, Belarus, Bulgaria, North Macedonia, Serbia and Romania.
boyla: noun. An Aboriginal witch doctor or sorcerer.
bra: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Braj.
bro: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Brokkat.
lab: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Linear A.
lap: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kabba-Laka.
lar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Larteh.
lay: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lama Bai.
lob: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lobi.
lobar: adjective. Of or relating to a lobe.
lobby: noun. An entryway or reception area; vestibule; passageway; corridor.
lop: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lopa.
lory: noun. Any of various small, brightly coloured parrots native to Australasia.
oar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Old Aramaic.
opal: noun. A mineral consisting, like quartz, of silica, but inferior to quartz in hardness and specific gravity, of the chemical formula Si O 2
ora: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oroha.
orb: noun. A spherical body; a sphere, especially one of the celestial spheres; a sun, planet, or star
orby: adjective. having characteristics of an orb
pal: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Middle Persian.
par: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Panamint.
pay: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pech.
payor: noun. One who makes a payment.
play: verb. To act in a manner such that one has fun; to engage in activities expressly for the purpose of recreation or entertainment.
ploy: noun. A tactic, strategy, or scheme.
pray: verb. To direct words, thoughts, or one's attention to a deity or any higher being, for the sake of adoration, thanks, petition for help,
pry: verb. To peer closely and curiously, especially at something closed or not public.
pya: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Burmese kyat.
pyrola: noun. Any of the genus Pyrola of wintergreens.
rap: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Rapa Nui.
ray: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rapa.
rob: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tae'.
ropy: adjective. Resembling rope in appearance or texture, used especially of muscles that are thick or hard to the touch.
royal: adjective. Of or relating to a monarch or his (or her) family.
rya: noun. A Scandinavian knotted-pile woollen rug, used as bed-cover and tapestry.
yap: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Yapese.
yar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yabarana.
yob: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yoba.
